_comment: WS-3.3 SPI registry — the machine-checked transcription of UL4600_SAFETY_CASE.md §5.2. Gated by kirra_verifier::spi_ledger (a root-crate test): every `emitted`/`derived` SPI's event_type literals must be actually emitted in src/ (scanned), a `parko`-scope SPI's verifier-side denominator must resolve, and the registry IDs must equal the §5.2 catalogue IDs. `threshold` is null where the §5 alarm limit is field-calibration TBD (the SPI is computed/reported but not alarm-gated); SPI-G06 (audit-chain integrity) is the hard 0-required precondition. Renaming/removing an audit event_type reds this gate. Reconciled to reality: SPI-L04 uses the real DETECTION_RANGE_UNTRUSTED (the catalogue's PERCEPTION_SNAPSHOT_UNHEALTHY / DETECTION_RANGE_DEGRADED were never emitted).

spis.name: Comparator divergence rate
spis.note: Latent systematic fault / state drift between the primary and diverse governors. Emitted in the parko node-local chain (parko-kirra COMPARATOR_DIVERGENCE_EVENT_TYPE); the verifier-side admitted-command denominator resolves here.

spis.name: RSS near-miss rate
spis.note: Closing on the minimum safe distance (IEEE 2846 / RSS). Emitted by append_rss_violation.

spis.name: Sensor-health fault rate
spis.note: Perception/sensor channel degrading toward trust loss; counted per node-hour.

spis.name: Perception-derate rate
spis.note: Validated detection range below the assured-clear floor (perception_monitor). Reconciled to the real emitted literal; a discrete snapshot-health event is not emitted today (§5.3 gap).

spis.name: Degraded-entry frequency
spis.note: ODD/operational stress forcing the reduced-capability posture. Derived: the posture-transition event filtered to (→ Degraded).

spis.name: Posture-flap / recovery-reset rate
spis.note: Instability: repeated fault↔recover cycling (hysteresis resets). Derived from the posture-transition + recovery-confirmed event pair.

spis.name: LockedOut / MRC engagement rate
spis.note: Safe-state fallback engaged. Derived: the posture-transition event filtered to (→ LockedOut). A discrete MRC-maneuver event remains a §5.3 gap.

spis.name: Command veto rate
spis.note: Governor blocked a command outright at the fabric/adapter layer (the kinematic-gateway veto rate is a §5.3 WCET-firewall gap).

spis.name: Command clamp rate
spis.note: Governor admitted but bounded a command at the fabric layer (gateway clamp rate is a §5.3 gap).

spis.name: Unknown-command denial count
spis.note: Malformed/unknown command reached the gate and was denied.

spis.name: Audit-chain integrity failures
spis.note: Tamper/corruption of the evidence base itself. Not a tally: evaluated from verify_audit_chain_full (chain_intact && signature_valid). 0 required — a failure invalidates the rollup window (§5.1).
